observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer:
a. in coordination with the former Acting Municipal Treasurer, submit to the
Municipal Accountant all the paid vouchers and payrolls, validated deposit slips, and other relevant documents needed to determine the nature and
purpose of unrecorded transactions identified in the Bank Reconciliation
Statements;
b. advise the former Acting Municipal Treasurer to fast track the preparation of
cashbooks and submit to the Audit Team the certified photocopies as soon as
possible;
c. prepare and submit a report showing full accounting of the bank deposits
made in CY 2024 and match them with the corresponding cash collections to
which they pertain, accompanied by a written justification for failure to ensure
proper matching thereof in the Report on Collections and Deposits; and
d. ensure that cash collections were deposited intact and not used for
disbursements and other purposes.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the bank
debits and credits identified by the AT otherwise provide details of the unrecorded
bank debits and credits to the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) develop accounting estimate for impairment of
receivables based on the management’s knowledge and experience on the
settlement of its receivables taking into account the age and collectability of
the balances,identified bad accounts eligible for write-off and other historical data
such as settlement/liquidation and collection experience; (ii) assess at the end of
each year or each reporting date whether there is evidence that the receivable
accounts are impaired; (iii) support the assessment, if possible, with evidence and
if lost, destroyed or cannot be produced follow the procedures of COA Circular
No. 93-404 dated October 18, 1993; (iv) calculate the allowance for impairment in
accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards or Philippine
Financial Reporting Standards and recognize the computed allowance in the
books to reflect the net realizable value of the receivable accounts in the Financial
Statements; and (v) submit to the Local Chief Executive the recommended list of
eligible accounts for write-off; and
b. Local Chief Executive (i) file a request for write-off of dormant accounts once
the COA reissue new guidelines and procedures but still continue to exert
effort to collect the written-off accounts; (ii) develop methods and strategies to
improve the collection and settlement of past due account; and (iii) ensure that
the MGC strictly adhere and implement installed policies and guidelines
covering receivable accounts.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant strictly follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in
recording the receipt and issuance of supplies in accordance with the
guidelines set forth in Section 114 Chapter 7 Volume 1 of COA Circular No.
2002-003 otherwise known as MNGAS for LGUs
b. Supply Officer (i) conduct physical count of inventories; (ii) maintain and
prepare the necessary documents in the receipt and issuance of inventories and
submit the documents to the Municipal Accountant as basis in recording in the
books; and (iii) prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report of the
Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and of supplies apply the prescribed
procedures, reporting and recording standards on receipts and
consumption/distribution of inventories as provided under IPSAS No. 12 and
MNGAS for LGUs.

a. Bids and Awards Committee-
 include/consolidate all supplemental PPMPs to Supplemental Annual
Procurement Plan in accordance to Section 7.1 and 7.2 of R-IRR of RA No. 9184ensuring that these PPMPs and APPs were carefully planned and scheduled to better manage the procurement, produce economical result, make the procurement procedures more efficient and practical;
 explain as to why the supplemental PPMP for the procurement of ACU for
Municipal Hall Building was not included in the supplemental APP;
 submit to the Audit Team the minutes of the meeting evidencing the
conduct of pre-procurement conference and pre-bid conference in
accordance with Section 20 and 22 of R-IRR of RA No. 9184: and
 strictly comply with the provisions of Section 35 and Annex “H” of R-IRR
of RA No. 9184 on the specific guidelines on the procedure of negotiated
procurement on two-failed biddings and all pertinent requirements and
procedures in the R-IRR of RA No. 9184 to adhere to the principles of
transparency, competitiveness, accountability, equity, efficiency, and
economy in its procurement process.
b. BAC/Technical Working Group provide document/report evidencing that BAC
has undertaken mandatory review and evaluation of the terms, conditions and
specifications in the bidding documents, including its cost estimates in accordance
with Section 35 of R-IRR of RA No. 9184; and
c. Municipal Engineer consider recomputing the liquidated damages incurred by the
supplier base on the actual inspection of the LGU inspectorate team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Inventory Committee (i) in coordination with the Municipal Accounting
Office, continue the reconciliation process between the inventory report and
accounting records; (ii) prepare the Physical Inventory Plan for the succeeding
inventory-taking activities and submit a copy to the Audit Team; (iii)
determine if the identified 13 lots are rightfully owned by the MGC before
conducting assessment of the fair value of the lots or else it should be dropped
from the books of the MGC; and (iv) henceforth, submit the RPCPPE to the
Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year;
b. the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to
derecognize the items below the ₱50.000.00 capitalization threshold; and
c. the Property Officer (i) carry out control procedures for the maintenance of
semi-expendable property records, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2022-004
and 2024-006; and assign the corresponding Property Number for all PPE
items for proper identification.

Additionally, we recommended that Management implement the “One-Time
Cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to
establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and
accountability.

We recommended that the Management:
a. submit to the Audit Team a (i) certificate issued by the Municipal Mayor on the
total amount of unencumbered savings generated from the cost-cutting and
systems improvement measures undertaken collectively by the Management and
its employees, and document that could prove that at least 70% of the planned
programs/activities/projects have been implemented and completed as of
September 30, 2023; and (ii) a resolution incorporating the guidelines/criteria for
granting CNA Incentive;
b. ensure that the cost-cutting measures and systems improvements indicated in
the CNA are directly attributable to the allowable MOOE items saved/earned
during the year and not just a mere reminder of a good conduct of a
government official/employee; and
c. ensure that grants of CNA Incentives are supported withcomplete and proper
supporting documents and in accordance with the DBM guidelines and COA
Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the BAC (i) prepare the PMR in its prescribed format of
GPPB Circular No. 03-2017 after the end of each semester for the approval of the
Municipal Mayor; (ii) submit/upload the same in the GPPB website in compliance
with Section 12.2 of the R-IRR of RA No. 9184; and (iii) furnish the Audit Team copies
of the PMRs for the 1st and 2nd quarter of CY 2024 in hard or electronic copies for
review and validation.

We recommended that the Management provide (i) at least 1% of the annual
budget appropriations for the Senior Citizen and Person with Disabilities fund and
not only based on National Tax Allotment; (ii) prepare and submit the LDPC and
LIPC to ensure the full strengthening and implementation of the LCPC; and (iii)
ensure optimal utilization of the fund to fully uphold the goal of addressing the
concerns of senior citizens and persons with disability and promotion of children’s
rights.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor, through the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer augment funds for debt-servicing under theGeneral
Fund to fully optimize the utilization of 20% LDF for current or new
development priorities as identified by the Local Development Council, in
order not to compromise the achievement of the desirable socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes beneficial to its
constituents; and
b) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Local Finance Committee
and Local Development Council maximize the utilization of fund resources by
allocating the 20% DF on fresh or new projects which will partake the nature of
investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the OIC-MDRRMO (i) stop the practice of charging not related
expenses to LDRRMF which do not support disaster risk reduction and
management projects and activities and (ii) strictly utilize the LDRRMF solely for
the programs and activities which encompass the four thematic areas of
disaster risk reduction and management pursuant to RA No. 10121 and
NDRRMC, DILG and DBM JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 to ensure
the availability of resources in mitigating the effects of calamities, and (iii)
monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs,
projects and activities listed in the LDRRMIP as incorporated in the local
development plans and work and financial plan to ensure full implementation of
the targeted plans and targets;
b. provide additional budgetary allocation from the regular appropriation for
the MDRRM Office for itsmaintenance and other operating expenditures and
capital outlay that are not related to DRRM projects and activities in
accordance with Section 4.3 of JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to ensure
the normal operation/function of the office and to void charging of
unnecessary expenses to the LDRRM Fund; and
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant torecordallpurchasestoitscorresponding
inventory account classification and to adjust this account for whatever
consumption on the basis of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
(SSMI) duly supported with the original copy of Requisition Issue Slip (RIS)
which should be prepared by the requisitioning office to be submitted weekly or
monthly to the Municipal Accountant pursuant to Section51,VolumeIof
MNGASforLGUs and Item 14 of IPSAS No. 12 on Inventories.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the GFPS Executive
Committee, (i) continue in promoting and ensuring gender mainstreaming in local
policy-making, planning, programming, budgeting, implementation, monitoring and
evaluation as required by JMC No. 2013-01, as amended, and other related GAD
laws, rules and regulations; (ii) prepare the corresponding Implementing Rules and
Regulations (IRR) in accordance with PCW MC No. 2009-1 dated September 16, 2009
and its subsequent enactment; and (iii)set GAD Agenda to be addressed by the LGU
during the three-year term of its leadership in accordance with the guidelines for
preparation set forth by PCW Memorandum Circular No. 2018-04 dated September
19, 2018.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) update all cashbooks, reconcile them with the subsidiary accounting records and submit a certified true copy to the Audit Team; and (b) henceforth, strictly observe daily updating of all cashbooks and monthly reconciliation with accounting records in accordance with Sections 30 and 31 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume II, and Section 63-F of the LTOM.
observations.reason: The Acting MT still failed update all her cashbooks, reconcile them with the subsidiary accounting records and submit certified true copy to the Audit Team allegedly to voluminous workload.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer:

submit a reasonable explanation for failure to deposit collections amounting to ₱8,640,956.11 despite the opportunity to do so on December 16, 2020;

deposit the amount of ₱8,640,956.11 to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury Account, copy furnish the Audit Team for verification and audit; and

henceforth, deposit regularly all municipal collections intact in accordance with Section 66 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I, or whenever there is opportunity to transact with the AGDB.
observations.reason: The Treasurer explained that she still needs to reconcile her cash books with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Mayor (i) require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report to the Inventory Committee for consolidation and subsequent verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; and (ii) direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Accounting Office, copy furnish the Audit Team; and (iii) consider the creation of a plantilla position for a GSO and the hiring of a qualified person for that position;

Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare the required property cards using the format provided in Appendix 54 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume II, and henceforth, maintain and regularly update them in accordance with Section 269 of the same Manual, Volume I; and

Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Inventory Committee and the Acting Treasurer, reconcile all property records and record adjustments to the PPE account balances, as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order No. 3, series of 2021, re-organizing the Inventory Committee wherein all department heads are now members.
observations.reason:
Despite the Management action in re-organizing its Inventory Committee, they still failed to conduct the annual physical count of properties and equipment, hence they were not able to prepare the required inventory report.

The Acting MT still failed to maintain the required property cards as of December 31, 2021, thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 3 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant apply the perpetual inventory method of accounting for supplies and materials and strictly adhere to the recording guidelines and procedures on the receipts of and consumption/ distribution of inventories as provided under Sections 188 to 190 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and

Municipal Mayor, require the Inventory Committee to conduct a semestral physical count of all supplies and materials and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnish to the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Committee failed to conduct physical count of all supplies and materials.

The Accountant still recorded supplies and materials as outright expenses upon delivery in CY 2021 thus, reiterated under Audit Observation No. 2 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
Acting Municipal Treasurer submit to the Municipal Accountant all the paid vouchers and payrolls, validated deposit slips; and other relevant documents needed to determine the nature and purpose of unrecorded transactions identified in the BRS; and
Municipal Accountant send a letter to the depository bank requesting for a copy of all the bank debit and credit memoranda for CY 2021 and exert more effort in obtaining sufficient documents needed in verifying and recording bank transactions.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) update all cashbooks, reconcile them with the subsidiary accounting records and then submit a certified true copy to the Audit Team for verification; and (b) henceforth, strictly observe daily updating of all cashbooks and monthly reconciliation with accounting records in accordance with Sections 26 and 27 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II, and Section 63-F of the LTOM.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit a report showing full accounting of the bank deposits made in CY 2021 totaling ₱72,389,574.66 and match them with the corresponding cash collections to which they pertain, accompanied by a written justification for failure to ensure proper matching thereof in the RCDs.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer deposit the full amount of ₱12,892,501.38 to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting thereof, accompanied by a satisfactory written justification for failure to deposit the same on December 31, 2021.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant strictly apply the prescribed recording standards on receipts and consumption/distribution of inventories as provided under Paragraphs 9 and 44 of IPSAS No. 12; and
Municipal Mayor, (i) require the Inventory Committee to reconcile the results of physical count of supplies and materials with the accounting records; and (ii) consider the creation of a plantilla position for a General Services Officer and the hiring of a qualified person for that position, subject to availability of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (i) require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; (ii) direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Audit Team through the Municipal Accountant for review and validation; and (iii) designate a Supply Officer who shall assist the Acting Municipal Treasurer in maintaining the property cards in accordance with Section 119 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, (a) through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, act with dispatch in obtaining certified true copies of the MOAs or other signed agreements covering the unused fund transfers from the NGAs totaling ₱18,683,193.64, by sending request letters to the respective source agencies, and thereafter cause the implementation of the PPAs accordingly;  and (b) submit copies thereof to the Audit Team, through the Municipal Accountant, for validation of the Account: Due to NGAs.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor , thru the Human Resources and Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant, submit the job orders, contracts of service, accomplishment reports and time records for the 425 wage payrolls totaling ₱14,771,833.05; and (b) Municipal Accountant, henceforth ensure that all wage payrolls are duly supported with complete documentation prior to its processing for approval and payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer and/or Municipal Accountant, obtain the original copy of sales invoices for the various supplies, materials and equipment procured in CY 2021 by sending letters to the concerned suppliers requiring submission thereof and forward them to the Audit Team through the Municipal Accountant for verification and audit.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant henceforth strictly observe the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 in reviewing the completeness and regularity of supporting documents for claims against the municipal funds.

We recommended that the Management obtain the required FDA and manufacturers’ certifications prescribed under Section 9.1.3.1 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions, prepare the lacking certifications of the requisitioning officers as to conformance with the current edition of the PNF Essential Medicines List, and submit them to the Audit Team through the Municipal Accountant for review and validation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant henceforth, ensure that all purchases of drugs and medicines are thoroughly reviewed as to completeness and propriety of supporting documents pursuant to Section 474.b(5) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Management submit the detailed computation of the ABC including the supporting documents/considerations used in computing the estimated unit cost for rapid antigen testing kits procured in CY 2021 and a written justification for the deficiencies noted on the foregoing audit observation.

We recommended further that the Municipal Mayor formulate and impose a policy on: (a) proper segregation of duties in the requisition of goods; and (b) observance of due diligence in the determination of price estimates of goods prior to commencing procurement.

We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer, in coordination with the other Department Heads, formulate a proposed comprehensive set of internal guidelines for granting uniform or clothing allowance pursuant to Section 12.0 of DBM BC No. 2018-1, to be adopted by the Sangguniang Bayan and for approval of the Municipal Mayor. Henceforth, ensure that all grants of clothing allowance are made in accordance with the said guidelines and other pertinent laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Management, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, submit a written justification for their use of Ordinance No. 2021-1868 as basis for budgetary realignment and disbursement of the amount of ₱4,544,644.00, instead of an appropriation ordinance covering a supplemental budget that was prepared, authorized and reviewed in accordance with the rules and procedures provided under Chapters 1, 2 and 3, Part II of the BOM for LGUs prescribed under LBC No. 112 dated June 10, 2016.

We recommended further that the Municipal Budget Officer henceforth, ensure that all claims against the local funds have existing and available fund appropriations based on a duly enacted annual or supplemental budget pursuant to Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the LDC, impose a policy for strict adherence to Section 109 of RA No. 7160 in carrying out their functions as mandated thereon and henceforth, ensure that priority development programs and projects are well-planned, procurement-and-implementation ready, and properly coordinated with the implementing offices and other agencies concerned.

We recommended that the Management commence the implementation of the continuing projects under the previous years’ LDRRMF at the soonest possible time and require the MDRRMO to submit detailed monthly reports on the status of their implementation.

We also recommended that the MDRRMO, with close coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, intensify monitoring and formulate strategies that will ensure prompt implementation of programs and projects funded under the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Management formulate a schedule indicating deadlines for preparing DV and issuance of checks for remittance of premium contributions, to be strictly followed by the Municipal Treasury and Accounting Offices and henceforth, ensure that all checks issued for remittance tally with the amount stated in PhilHealth’s Statement of Premium Accounts.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of PhilHealth premium contributions of previous years, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate.

We recommended that the:
Acting Municipal Treasurer promptly submit the disbursement vouchers and reports to the Municipal Accountant so that all taxes withheld during the month are included in the Monthly Alphalist of Payees and remitted to the BIR; and
Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer, (i) cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid possible interest, penalties and surcharges for late remittances, otherwise they shall be personally liable for the surcharge and interest that may be imposed thereon; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld on contractors and suppliers and compensation of Municipal officials and employees are remitted intact within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) submit written explanation for not using the proper format in reporting bank deposits for CY 2020; and (b) henceforth, report all bank deposits in the RCD in accordance with Section 63, Volume I, and Section 41, Volume II, of the GAM for LGUs in order to check the proper matching of collections and deposits.
observations.managementAction: The Treasurer has started to use RCD to report collections and bank deposits.
observations.reason: Bank deposits made in CY 2021 were not properly matched in collections in the RCD because the Treasurer failed to properly use the prescribed reporting formats, thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 1 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) prepare the journal entries to record the necessary adjustments for reconciling items totalling ₱12,077,173.45; and (b) henceforth, strictly observe the monthly preparation and submission of BRS for all cash in bank accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant submitted the BRS for cash in back accounts and prepared the necessary adjusting entries on several reconciling items.
observations.reason: The Accountant failed prepared all the necessary adjustments due to failure of the Treasurer to submit all disbursement vouchers and reports as basis to record reconciling items, thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 3 in Part II of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

OIC - MPDC prepare a complete monitoring report on the status of implementation of Government PPAs as of December 31, 2020, including all PPAs funded out of the General Fund, 20% DF current and continuing appropriations, Bayanihan Grant on Cities and Municipalities, Special Education Fund, Trust Fund and other fund sources; and

Municipal Engineer assist the MPDC in monitoring the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality, regardless of fund source.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted monitoring reports on the status of implementation of Government PPAs in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Engineer ensure that all proposed infrastructure projects undergo PERT/CPM analysis and observe diligence in the preparation of the Program of Work by filling in all the necessary information about the projects, including the target starting date and estimated number of calendar days to complete; and

Municipal Accountant ensure that all payments for infrastructure projects are supported with a duly accomplished and approved Program of Works and a PERT/CPM diagram as required under Section 3, Annex “A” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
The Engineer ensured that all infrastructure projects undergo PERT/CPM analysis and observed diligence in the preparation of Program of Work. 

The Accountant is now implementing stringent measures and carefully evaluating the supporting documents so that payments will only be processed if infrastructure projects’ requirement are complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MSWDO submit to the Audit Team a copy of the following documents:

Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the Municipality and the DSWD pursuant to Section III.A.1 of DSWD MC No. 2020-006;

For each beneficiary, a copy of updated SAC Form, photo taken during actual receipt, and specific documentary requirements as enumerated under Section VII.A.1 of DSWD MC No. 2020-009 or a certification from the barangay that the beneficiary is in need of assistance from DSWD, as the case may be; and

Reasonable explanation why the lacking documents were not submitted to the Audit Team and why some claimants were allowed to receive the cash assistance even without a proper authorization letter.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant henceforth, ensure that cash assistance payrolls under all social amelioration programs are duly supported with complete documentation in accordance with pertinent DSWD circulars and other related issuances.
observations.managementAction:
The MSWDO submitted a copy of MOA, SAC Forms and photos.

The Accountant is now implementing stringent measures and carefully evaluating supporting documents when it comes to programs like cash assistance.
observations.reason: The submitted copy of MOA was undated and was not signed by  the DSWD Regional Director while the SAC Forms and photos are not complete. This was already communicated to the Management however, no response was received  from the Management thus far.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MDRRMO, in coordination with the MDRRMC, (i) prepare the required investment plan for the LDRRMF-STF amounting to ₱13,476,032.59; the investment plan should correspond to the actual expenditures incurred in CY 2020 totaling ₱12,829,663.00 and should be accompanied by a written justification on why it was not formulated prior to actual disbursement of the fund; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that all proposed programs, projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF are duly incorporated in the annual LDRRMFIP and integrated in the approved AIP of the Municipality; and

OIC-MPDC, in coordination with the LDC and upon preparation of the required investment plan by the MDRRMO, prepare a Supplemental AIP therefor and submit it along with the MDRRMO’s justification to the Sangguniang Bayan for their consideration and approval should they find it reasonable based on the current circumstances.
observations.managementAction: The Management prepared a supplemental AIP for projects funded out of CY 2020 LDRRMF that were transferred to the STF in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The Management has not yet prepared the investment plan that correspond to the expenditures incurred in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant (i) prepare the adjusting journal entries to recognize the receipt of BGCM amounting to ₱7,828,274.00; (ii) submit reasonable explanation for her failure to provide separate journals and subsidiary ledger for the BGCM; and (iii) submit to the Office of the Mayor a detailed report on the receipts and utilization of BGCM, copy furnish the Audit Team, to facilitate the preparation of the monthly reports on fund utilization and status of implementation as required under Section 4.2 of LBC No. 125; 

Municipal Budget Officer submit (i) copy of all registries of appropriations, allotments and obligations for CY 2020, including that of the special account for BGCM; and (ii) reasonable explanation for presenting the BGCM as part of the regular budget of the Office of the Mayor in the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations; and

Municipal Mayor, through the Local Finance Committee (LFC), cause the immediate preparation and posting of monthly reports on fund utilization and status of implementation of programs, projects and activities out of the BGCM pursuant to Sections 4.2 and 4.3 of LBC No. 125 for transparency purposes and submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction:
The Management submitted detailed fund utilization report of BGCM and copy of all registries of appropriations, allotment.

The Accountant prepared the adjusting journal entries to recognize the receipt of BGCM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Acting Human Resource Management Officer submit (i) a copy of duly accomplished monthly time records and approved DTRs of municipal employees and personnel who received Covid-19 Hazard Pay from March 17 to April 30, 2020; and (ii) office orders authorizing the municipal employees and personnel to physically report for work in their respective offices or work stations on the prescribed hours during the implementation of ECQ; 

Municipal Mayor submit a reasonable explanation for allowing the direct payment of Covid-19 Hazard Pay to barangay officials and personnel, uniformed employees from the BFP, and frontline workers from the PPA; and

Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims for hazard pay or other personnel benefits are in accordance with pertinent rules and guidelines and duly supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted a copy of duly accomplished monthly time records and approved DTRs of municipal employees and personnel from March 17 to April 30, 2020 and an explanation for allowing the direct payment of Covid-19 Hazard Pay to barangay officials and personnel.
observations.reason: The Management failed to provide office orders authorizing the municipal employees and personnel to physically report for work in their respective offices.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

OIC - Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator exercise prudence in the formulation of the development plans and policies for consideration of the Municipal Development Council in coming up with a comprehensive local development plan that is compliant with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020;

Municipal Accountant, henceforth, ensure that all claims charged to the 20% DF are: (i) supported with a duly approved Program of Work and detailed accomplishment report establishing the nature of works performed as a capital expenditure; and (ii) not among those prohibited under Section 4.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and Section 2.2 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020; and

Acting Municipal Treasurer submit: (i) reasonable explanation on why her claim for reimbursement of travel expense amounting to ₱50,000.00 was charged to the 20% DF; and (ii) justification for lack of valid supporting documents to support her claim for travel.
observations.managementAction:
The MPDC made all necessary adjustment in the formulation of development plans and policies.

The Accountant ensured that all claims charged to 20% DF are duly supported and in accordance with the provided rules and guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) exert all the necessary efforts to locate the unsubmitted documents and cause the immediate submission thereof to the Audit Team; and (b) henceforth, ensure that all financial transactions recorded in the books of accounts are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period in order to enable the complete and timely review and validation of accounts and financial transactions of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the twelve check disbursement vouchers to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairperson, through the BAC Secretariat (a) submit a copy of all contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2020 either in printed or electronic form, whichever is more convenient or cost-efficient; and (b) henceforth observe regular submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team in such manner and timing as may be deemed reasonable in view of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction:
The Management regularly submitted list of purchase orders in CY 2021.

The Management also submitted 21 contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2021.
observations.reason: The Management failed to submit all contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2020 because they are still retrieving and compiling the said documents.

observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer submitted the official receipt issued by the Thea Miel Pharma Plus.

The Municipal Mayor is imposing strict adherence on the use of transaction forms prescribed with emphasis on the application of control numbers and indication of date on the Acceptance and Inspection Reports.
observations.reason:
The Management did not submit a certification that all the drugs and medicines requested and procured in CY 2020 fall within and confirm with the current edition of the PNF.

Similar deficiency was observed on procured drugs and medicines in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

BAC Chairperson, thru the Secretariat, submit to the Audit Team (i) relevant office orders authorizing their claims for honoraria, (ii) copy of minutes and attendance sheets of BAC meetings relative to procurement projects awarded in CY 2020, (iii) certification on the conduct of competitive bidding for each procurement project for which honoraria were claimed; and (iv) copy of the CY 2020 APP and all pertinent amendments thereto; and

Municipal Accountant, henceforth, diligently ensure that all claims for honoraria are supported with complete documentation in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction:
According to the BAC Chairperson, the documents enumerated in the audit recommendation were already compiled.

The Accountant ensured that all claims of honoraria are supported with complete documentation.
observations.reason: The documents remained in the custody of the BAC Secretariat and were not yet transmitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an order to stop the payment of incentives to national government officials assigned to the Municipality until the SB has passed a resolution authorizing the grant of such incentives and prescribing uniform guidelines on the allowable rates and documentary requirements to be imposed.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that no claim for incentives of national government officials shall be processed unless it is duly supported with an SB resolution authorizing its grant and is in accordance with prescribed guidelines as to allowable rates and documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: The SB passed Ordinance No. 2021-1871 authorizing grant of monthly honoraria to different heads of National Government Agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Acting Municipal Treasurer submit promptly the disbursement vouchers and reports to the Municipal Accountant so that all taxes withheld during the month are included in the Monthly Alphalist of Payees and remitted to the BIR; and

Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer, (i) cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid possible interest, penalties and surcharges for late remittances, otherwise they shall be personally liable for the surcharge and interest that may be imposed; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld on contractors and suppliers and compensation of Municipal officials and employees are remitted intact within the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended.
observations.managementAction: The Management remitted all unremitted withholding tax of CY 2020.
observations.reason: The Treasurer still failed to promptly submit the disbursement vouchers to the Accountant resulting in unremitted taxes in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

upon receipt of the AAR for CY 2020, direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and

henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to submit AAPSI on the observations and recommendations included in the CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) submit written explanation for continuously not using the proper forms in reporting bank deposits for CY 2019; and (b) henceforth, report all bank deposits in Section B of the RCD in accordance with Section 32, Volume I and Section 49, Volume II of the NGAS Manual in order to check proper matching of collections and deposits.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to submit a written explanation for continuously not using the proper forms in reporting bank deposits and still failed to observe proper reporting of bank deposits during CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Mayor, through the Inventory Committee, require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit the report thereon to the Inventory Committee for verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; and

Municipal Treasurer prepare Property Cards per category of PPE and henceforth maintain the same in accordance with Section 119 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order No. 03, s. 2021, reorganizing the Municipal Inventory Committee in order to fast track the conduct of physical count of PPE and preparation of inventory reports.
observations.reason: The Municipal Inventory Committee still failed to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE as of December 31, 2020 and the Municipal Treasurer failed to prepare property cards per category of PPE,thus reiterated under Audit Observation No. 2in Part II of this AAR.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order No. 03, s. 2021, reorganizing the Municipal Inventory Committee in order to fast track the conduct of physical count of PPE and preparation of inventory reports.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Inventory Committee failed to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE as of December 31, 2020.

The Municipal Engineer failed to prepare the Report on Local Road Network.

The Municipal Treasurer failed to prepare Road Network Property Cards.

observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The former OIC-Municipal Engineer and the MPDC has not yet submitted a justification for signing the recommendation on the certificates of inspection and completion despite lack of inspection report, STE and As-Built Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer (a) submit a reasonable explanation for signing the checks and releasing payment for DVs and payrolls totaling ₱6,167,464.34 despite lack of certification as to availability of funds and the LCE’s approval; and (b) henceforth, ensure that no disbursement of government funds shall be made without the complete certification and approval of proper officials pursuant to Section 4.5 of PD No. 1445 and Section 344 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer failed to submit a reasonable explanation for signing the checks and releasing payment for DVs and payrolls stated in the audit observation despite lack of certification as to availability of funds and the LCE’s approval.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Treasurer, being the officer accountable for the cash advance, submit: (i) valid AIR and ARE to document acceptance and inspection of and accountability for the Blood Chemistry Analyzer; (ii) certification from the Municipal Health Officer that the Blood Chemistry Analyzer purchased may be accepted as a reasonable alternative for the equipment supposedly  requested; and (iii) written justification for drawing the cash advance and authorizing the former Municipal Mayor to encash the check despite the questionable nature of the said cash advance; Otherwise, refund the amount of ₱425,500.00 to settle the cash advance;

Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Accountant, ensure the immediate settlement of the cash advance by issuing a demand letter to the Accountable Officer, copy furnished the Audit Team; and

Municipal Vice-Mayor/then Mayor submit a satisfactory written explanation why he allowed the cash advance to be transferred to him and then performed the disbursing function.

We also recommended that, henceforth, the (i) requesting officials observe the rules and regulations in the preparation of PR; (ii) BAC Chairperson and members be mindful of their duties and responsibilities in the procurement of goods in accordance with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (iii) local officials concerned see that the PO contain all the basic requirements pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996; and (iv) accountable officers observe strictly the guidance in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002, as amended.
observations.managementAction: On-going preparation of the demand letter to accountable officers concerned.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Vice-Mayor failed to submit the documents stated in the audit recommendation.

The cash advance stated in the audit observation remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) submit a satisfactory written explanation for the delayed reporting of community tax collections totaling ₱203,908.00; (b) impose proper segregation of duties and functions in the Municipal Treasury Office in accordance with Section 50 of the GAAM Volume III; and (c) henceforth, ensure prompt reporting of all municipal collections at the close of each working day in accordance with Section 29 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
The Municipal Treasurer did not submit a written explanation for the delayed reporting of community tax collections in CY 2019.

We did not receive any feedback regarding the imposition of proper segregation of duties and functions in the Treasury Office and the prompt reporting of collections for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) propose to the Sangguniang Bayan the adoption of the LEE Manual as recommended under LBC No. 111 dated June 10, 2016; (b) assign or designate the relevant local officials who will be responsible in planning for and implementing the new LEEs; and (c) enjoin the said officials to conduct an evaluation on the operations of the municipal public market, slaughterhouse, hospital, cemetery and commercial/office spaces in accordance with Section 4 of the LEE Manual.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The Municipality has a proposed plan of building its Municipal Complex, thus once the LGU has established the said complex it will work on the improvement of its existing local enterprises and will delve into the integration of LEE Manual.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MPDC formulate integrated economic, social, physical and other developmental plans and policies for consideration of the Municipal Development Council in coming up with a comprehensive local development plan that is compliant with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1;

Municipal Development Council exercise prudence in the formulation of the development plans and public investment programs to ensure that the 20% DF is used solely for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; and

Municipal Accountant, henceforth, ensure that all disbursements out of the 20% DF are (i) supported with a duly approved Program of Work or other documentation that would establish the nature of the expenditure as investment or capital outlay and (ii) not among the list of expenditure items which are prohibited under Section 4.0 of the same JMC.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason:
Various administrative expenses were charged to the 20% Development Fund 
in CY 2020 and thus reiterated in Audit Observation No. 12 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MDRRMO (i) submit a summary report on the distribution of relief goods and supplies for CY 2019, duly supported with distribution forms duly acknowledged by the beneficiaries; and (ii) initiate the preparation of a supplemental investment plan for the utilization of the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF;

Municipal Accountant (i) submit an explanation why the payments for relief goods totaling ₱133,650.00 were drawn from the STF despite lack of investment plan for the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF; (ii) prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for all relief goods and supplies; and (iii) draw the necessary journal entries to account for the remaining relief goods and supplies, if any; and

Municipal Treasurer, as the Property and Supply Officer, prepare Stock Cards for all inventory items and henceforth observe proper monitoring of acquisition and distribution of relief goods and supplies.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The Management failed to prepare and submit the reports and documents stated in the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer (a) submit a written explanation on why taxes withheld were not promptly remitted intact to the BIR; (b) cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid possible interests, penalties and surcharges for late payments; and (c) ensure, henceforth, that taxes withheld are remitted intact and on the prescribed period pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 dated April 17, 1998, as amended.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted an explanation for the delayed remittance of withholding taxes and exerted efforts to address the deficiency. However, the Treasurer failed to submit explanation on her part.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2020, withholding taxes were still not remitted intact on the prescribed period as discussed in Audit Observation No. 16 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) submit written explanation for not using the proper format in reporting bank deposits for CY 2020; and (b) henceforth, report all bank deposits in the RCD in accordance with Section 63, Volume I, and Section 41, Volume II, of the GAM for LGUs in order to check the proper matching of collections and deposits.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) prepare the journal entries to record the necessary adjustments for reconciling items totalling ₱12,077,173.45; and (b) henceforth, strictly observe the monthly preparation and submission of BRS for all cash in bank accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) update all cashbooks, reconcile them with the subsidiary accounting records and submit a certified true copy to the Audit Team; and (b) henceforth, strictly observe daily updating of all cashbooks and monthly reconciliation with accounting records in accordance with Sections 30 and 31 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume II, and Section 63-F of the LTOM.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer:
submit a reasonable explanation for failure to deposit collections amounting to ₱8,640,956.11 despite the opportunity to do so on December 16, 2020;
deposit the amount of ₱8,640,956.11 to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury Account, copy furnish the Audit Team for verification and audit; and
henceforth, deposit regularly all municipal collections intact in accordance with Section 66 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I, or whenever there is opportunity to transact with the AGDB.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor (i) require all the Department Heads to conduct a complete physical count of all PPE under their respective offices and submit a report to the Inventory Committee for consolidation and subsequent verification and reconciliation with the accounting records; and (ii) direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE and submit the same to the Accounting Office, copy furnish the Audit Team; and (iii) consider the creation of a plantilla position for a GSO and the hiring of a qualified person for that position;
Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare the required property cards using the format provided in Appendix 54 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume II, and henceforth, maintain and regularly update them in accordance with Section 269 of the same Manual, Volume I; and
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Inventory Committee and the Acting Treasurer, reconcile all property records and record adjustments to the PPE account balances, as necessary.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant apply the perpetual inventory method of accounting for supplies and materials and strictly adhere to the recording guidelines and procedures on the receipts of and consumption/distribution of inventories as provided under Sections 188 to 190 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I; and
Municipal Mayor, require the Inventory Committee to conduct a semestral physical count of all supplies and materials and submit a report thereon to the Municipal Accountant, copy furnish to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the:
OIC - MPDC prepare a complete monitoring report on the status of implementation of Government PPAs as of December 31, 2020, including all PPAs funded out of the General Fund, 20% DF current and continuing appropriations, Bayanihan Grant on Cities and Municipalities, Special Education Fund, Trust Fund and other fund sources; and
Municipal Engineer assist the MPDC in monitoring the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality, regardless of fund source.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Engineer ensure that all proposed infrastructure projects undergo PERT/CPM analysis and observe diligence in the preparation of the Program of Work by filling in all the necessary information about the projects, including the target starting date and estimated number of calendar days to complete; and
Municipal Accountant ensure that all payments for infrastructure projects are supported with a duly accomplished and approved Program of Works and a PERT/CPM diagram as required under Section 3, Annex “A” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the MSWDO submit to the Audit Team a copy of the following documents:
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the Municipality and the DSWD pursuant to Section III.A.1 of DSWD MC No. 2020-006;
For each beneficiary, a copy of updated SAC Form, photo taken during actual receipt, and specific documentary requirements as enumerated under Section VII.A.1 of DSWD MC No. 2020-009 or a certification from the barangay that the beneficiary is in need of assistance from DSWD, as the case may be; and
Reasonable explanation why the lacking documents were not submitted to the Audit Team and why some claimants were allowed to receive the cash assistance even without a proper authorization letter.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant henceforth, ensure that cash assistance payrolls under all social amelioration programs are duly supported with complete documentation in accordance with pertinent DSWD circulars and other related issuances.

We recommended that the:
MDRRMO, in coordination with the MDRRMC,(i) prepare the required investment plan for the LDRRMF-STF amounting to ₱13,476,032.59; the investment plan should correspond to the actual expenditures incurred in CY 2020 totaling ₱12,829,663.00 and should be accompanied by a written justification on why it was not formulated prior to actual disbursement of the fund; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that all proposed programs, projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balance of prior years’ LDRRMF are duly incorporated in the annual LDRRMFIP and integrated in the approved AIP of the Municipality; and
OIC-MPDC, in coordination with the LDC and upon preparation of the required investment plan by the MDRRMO, prepare a Supplemental AIP therefor and submit it along with the MDRRMO’s justification to the Sangguniang Bayan for their consideration and approval should they find it reasonable based on the current circumstances.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant (i) prepare the adjusting journal entries to recognize the receipt of BGCM amounting to ₱7,828,274.00; (ii) submit reasonable explanation for her failure to provide separate journals and subsidiary ledger for the BGCM; and (iii) submit to the Office of the Mayor a detailed report on the receipts and utilization of BGCM, copy furnish the Audit Team, to facilitate the preparation of the monthly reports on fund utilization and status of implementation as required under Section 4.2 of LBC No. 125;
Municipal Budget Officer submit (i) copy of all registries of appropriations, allotments and obligations for CY 2020, including that of the special account for BGCM; and (ii) reasonable explanation for presenting the BGCM as part of the regular budget of the Office of the Mayor in the Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations; and
Municipal Mayor, through the Local Finance Committee (LFC), cause the immediate preparation and posting of monthly reports on fund utilization and status of implementation of programs, projects and activities out of the BGCM pursuant to Sections 4.2 and 4.3 of LBC No. 125 for transparency purposes and submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the:
Acting Human Resource Management Officer submit (i) a copy of duly accomplished monthly time records and approved DTRs of municipal employees and personnel who received Covid-19 Hazard Pay from March 17 to April 30, 2020; and (ii) office orders authorizing the municipal employees and personnel to physically report for work in their respective offices or work stations on the prescribed hours during the implementation of ECQ;
Municipal Mayor submit a reasonable explanation for allowing the direct payment of Covid-19 Hazard Pay to barangay officials and personnel, uniformed employees from the BFP, and frontline workers from the PPA; and
Municipal Accountant ensure that all claims for hazard pay or other personnel benefits are in accordance with pertinent rules and guidelines and duly supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that the:
OIC - Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator exercise prudence in the formulation of the development plans and policies for consideration of the Municipal Development Council in coming up with a comprehensive local development plan that is compliant with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and DILG-DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020;
Municipal Accountant, henceforth, ensure that all claims charged to the 20% DF are: (i) supported with a duly approved Program of Work and detailed accomplishment report establishing the nature of works performed as a capital expenditure; and (ii) not among those prohibited under Section 4.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and Section 2.2 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020; and
Acting Municipal Treasurer submit: (i) reasonable explanation on why her claim for reimbursement of travel expense amounting to ₱50,000.00 was charged to the 20% DF; and (ii) justification for lack of valid supporting documents to support her claim for travel.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) exert all the necessary efforts to locate the unsubmitted documents and cause the immediate submission thereof to the Audit Team; and (b) henceforth, ensure that all financial transactions recorded in the books of accounts are submitted to the Audit Team within the prescribed period in order to enable the complete and timely review and validation of accounts and financial transactions of the Municipality for CY 2020.

We recommended that the BAC Chairperson, through the BAC Secretariat (a) submit a copy of all contracts and purchase orders executed in CY 2020 either in printed or electronic form, whichever is more convenient or cost-efficient; and (b) henceforth observe regular submission of contracts and purchase orders to the Audit Team in such manner and timing as may be deemed reasonable in view of the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the:
BAC Chairperson, thru the Secretariat, submit to the Audit Team (i) relevant office orders authorizing their claims for honoraria, (ii) copy of minutes and attendance sheets of BAC meetings relative to procurement projects awarded in CY 2020, (iii) certification on the conduct of competitive bidding for each procurement project for which honoraria were claimed; and (iv) copy of the CY 2020 APP and all pertinent amendments thereto; and
Municipal Accountant, henceforth, diligently ensure that all claims for honoraria are supported with complete documentation in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an order to stop the payment of incentives to national government officials assigned to the Municipality until the SB has passed a resolution authorizing the grant of such incentives and prescribing uniform guidelines on the allowable rates and documentary requirements to be imposed.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that no claim for incentives of national government officials shall be processed unless it is duly supported with an SB resolution authorizing its grant and is in accordance with prescribed guidelines as to allowable rates and documentary requirements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
upon receipt of the AAR for CY 2020, direct the Department Heads concerned to immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and
henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) submit a reasonable explanation for failure to deposit collections totaling ₱7,599,456.38 despite having been to the bank on December 16, 2019; (b) deposit the amount of ₱7,599,456.38 to the AGDB and prepare a report showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury Account; and (c) henceforth, deposit all municipal collections intact strictly in accordance with Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 whenever there is opportunity to transact with the AGDB.
observations.managementAction: Collection of advance payment from real properties that was reported in December 2019 were deposited in January 2020.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to submit a reasonable explanation for failure to deposit collections and submit a report showing full accounting of the Cash in Local Treasury account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Treasurer immediately submit to the Municipal Accountant (i) all the paid vouchers and payrolls; (ii) copy of validated deposit slips; and (iii) other relevant documents to determine the nature and purpose of unrecorded transactions reflected in the bank statements; and

Municipal Accountant (i) send a letter to the depository bank requesting for a copy of all the bank debit and credit memoranda for CY 2019; and (ii) exert more effort in obtaining sufficient documents to be used as basis for verification and recording of bank transactions.
observations.managementAction: Accounts were verified and tallied with records of the Municipal Treasurer and some necessary adjustments were already effected.
observations.reason: Several bank transactions and adjustments still remained unrecorded in the books of accounts as of December 31, 2020 due to late preparation of the bank reconciliation statements for the months of April to December 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) exert efforts to update all cashbooks, reconcile them with the subsidiary accounting records and then submit a certified true copy to the Audit Team; and (b) henceforth, strictly observe daily updating of all cashbooks and monthly reconciliation with accounting records in accordance with Sections 26 and 27 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II, and Section 63-F of the LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted to the Audit Team the certified true copies of cashbooks as of December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer failed to update all cashbooks, reconcile with the subsidiary recordsfor CY 2020.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer explained that the time extension orders issued by the Management were actually time suspension orders in substance, and she presented substantial documentation to support their validity. She alsoprepared an updated computation of liquidated damages,inspection report and statement of time elapsed and recommended to the Municipal Mayor to terminate the contract of the two projects stated in the audit observation.

Relative thereto, the Municipal Mayor issued Executive Order No. 73, series of 2020, organizing the Municipal Contract Termination Review Committee.

The Municipal Accountant imposed stringent measures in reviewing payments to contractors.

While the former Municipal Mayor failed to submit the required explanation, the Municipal Engineer has already provided adequate basis for the approval of requests for time suspension dated January 14 and May 6, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, observe utmost diligence in recording transactions to ensure strict adherence with the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 as well as the fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant exerted due diligence in the recording of prizes for cultural and athletic activities for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

BAC (i) submit a reasonable explanation for failure to disqualify the bidders who submitted quotations higher than the ABC, and (ii) henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR to ensure that all purchases of the Municipality go through the correct procurement process; and

Municipal Accountant, henceforth, ensure that all purchases are duly supported with complete and proper documentation, including the duly signed ABC, APP or excerpts thereof showing the subject goods to be procured, all the relevant BAC resolutions, duly accomplished RFQs containing complete information, abstract of bids/quotation and evaluation report on bids/price quotations, among others.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant imposed stringent measures in reviewing vouchers and supporting documents for CY 2020.
observations.reason: The BAC has not yet submitted a reasonable explanation for failure to disqualify the bidder submitted quotations higher than the ABC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Provincial Government of Palawan to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursement pertaining to the remaining ₱75,000.00; and (b) based on the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursement, issue demand letter requesting for the return of any unutilized balance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already issued a demand letter to the Provincial Government of Palawan for the return of the unutilized balance of the fund transfer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MPDC prepare a complete monitoring report on the status of implementation of government PPAs as of December 31, 2019, including other programs and activities under the General Fund, projects under the 20% DF continuing appropriations and PPAs funded by national government agencies and other fund sources; and

Municipal Engineer assist the MPDC in monitoring the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality, regardless of fund source.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the MPDC submitted monthly reports on the status of implementation of projects/ program/ activities funded by the CY 2019 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: The submitted monitoring reports on the status of implementation of government PPAs did not include information about projects funded by current appropriations, continuing appropriations from CY 2015 to 2018, and those funded under the Special Education Fund and Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

MPDC intensify the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of development projects and henceforth submit quarterly reports on the same with recommendations for management actions on projects with unsatisfactory work accomplishments;

Municipal Engineer henceforth conduct regular inspection of all municipal public works and submit a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects to the Mayor for monitoring purposes; and

Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF to achieve the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer and the MPDC submitted monthly report on the status of implementation of projects/ program/ activities funded by the CY 2019 20% DF.

The Management exerted due efforts ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer initiate the transfer of the amount of ₱627,964.00 from the General Fund bank account to the STF depository account for LDRRMF and then submit the necessary documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended balance of LDRRMF for CY 2018 has already been transferred to the STF depository account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of the unremitted balance of the Due to PhilHealth account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate; and

Municipal Treasurer, (i) based on the analysis prepared by the Municipal Accountant, remit to the PhilHealth all contributions from prior periods so that only those from the preceding month shall remain as balance of Due to PhilHealth account; and (ii) henceforth, ensure the prompt remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the period prescribed under PhilHealth Circular No. 0001, s. 2014 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.
observations.managementAction: Unremitted balance of Due to PhilHealth account totaling ₱82,117.41 was remitted on January 2020, inclusive of penalties.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of Due to PhilHealth account is still on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant prepare an analysis of the unremitted balance of the Due to GSIS account, reconcile billings and remittances and effect necessary adjustments and corrections, as appropriate; and

Municipal Treasurer, (i) based on the analysis prepared by the Municipal Accountant, remit to the GSIS all contributions and/or loan amortizations from prior periods so that only those from the preceding month shall remain as the balance of Due to GSIS account; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that GSIS contributions and loan amortization are remitted intact within the prescribed period under RA No. 8291 for the welfare of the employees and to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.
observations.managementAction: The analysis of the discrepancy and unremitted balances of the Due to GSIS account are being updated and given appropriate actions by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of Due to GSIS account and necessary adjustments are still on process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

Municipal Engineer: (i) submit a computation of liquidated damages on the BUB and ADM projects in accordance with Section 8, Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; (ii) submit a report on negative slippages incurred by the contractor and if warranted, issue a recommendation to the Municipal Mayor to forfeit the performance security, suspend any payment due and disqualify the contractor from participating in biddings for future projects; and (iii) henceforth ensure adequate supervision on all municipal public works and submit a monthly report on the status of implementation of all infrastructure projects;
 
BAC conduct an investigation on the incomplete disclosure of information on the contractor’s list of ongoing projects and if warranted, cause the filing of a case against the contractor for concealment of information in the eligibility requirements pursuant to Section 65.3 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and

Municipal Accountant cause the immediate submission of the following documents for the BUB and ADM projects: (i) MOA or Trust Agreement; (ii) copy of official receipt evidencing the fund transfer; (iii) photocopy of vouchers of all previous payments; (iv) As-Built Plans; (v) Statement of Time Elapsed; and (vi) Warranty Security.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The Management failed to give ample consideration on the audit recommendation. In CY 2019, retention money for the ADM project was released without imposition of liquidated damages, as discussed under Audit Observation No. 6 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of all programmed project and activities charged against the 20% DF to achieve effectively and efficiently its socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and submit the monthly monitoring report on government PPAs as required under COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Insufficient manpower with technical expertise to prepare Program of Works and supervise infrastructure works.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF amounting to ₱627,964.00 to the respective Trust Fund bank account and make the necessary journal entries in the books.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer still failed to transfer the unexpended balance to the Special Trust Fund account as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO, as the executive arm of the LDRRMC, coordinate with the BAC and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of the programs/projects under the LDRRMF. Henceforth, ensure that all projects appropriated in the LDRRMF are implemented during the year it was appropriated in order to increase the capacity of the Municipality to cope with potential hazards and disasters.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Insufficient manpower and lack of proper coordination with other concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

through the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committees, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the Municipality by educating their constituents through Information Education Campaign on the proper segregation of waste and by ensuring that MRF are operating efficiently;

through the MSWMB, observe proper waste collection like imposition of “No Segregation No Collection Policy”, placing trash bins with proper labels on designated waste collection areas and collect only those waste which are properly segregated. Furthermore, coordinate with NSWMC, PCSD and other government agencies for the safe closure of the controlled dumpsite and for the conversion of compliant solid waste disposal facility; and

through the MPDC, ensure implementation of SWM programs and projects during the year and previous years to achieve favorable environmental outcome and prevent hazardous effect of pollution and global warming. Moreover, ensure the optimal utilization of fund appropriated for SWM in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Lack of specific individual/ organization to implement the SWMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the immediate submission of the documents and reports pertaining to revenue-generating projects for CY 2018.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance of taxes withheld to the BIR to avoid possible interest/penalty for late payments and/or possible misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2019, withholding taxes were still not remitted intact on the prescribed period as discussed in Audit Observation No. 18 in this AAR.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure the prompt remittance of GSIS contributions within the period prescribed under RA No. 8291 to avoid incurrence of unnecessary interest and penalties.
